module_map.responsibility: HTTP protocol layer: request parsing, response serialization, headers, cookies, query parameters, POST/file uploads
URL routing and pattern matching: resolve incoming HTTP paths to view functions, reverse URL generation from view names
View handlers: generic views (class-based), error views (404/500), static file serving, debug utilities
HTTP request/response processing pipeline: security (CSRF, CSP), compression, caching, locale negotiation
Template rendering: engine initialization, context building, tag/filter loading, template caching
Form rendering and validation: model forms, widgets, field validation, form error handling
Database abstraction layer: connection management, transaction support, ORM query interface
Core utilities: exceptions, validators, signing, pagination, settings resolution
Utility functions and data structures: text encoding, HTML escaping, caching, deprecation helpers, tree structures
Signal/event dispatcher for decoupled communication between Django components

pipeline_trace.description: WSGI/ASGI environ dict parsed into HttpRequest object; headers extracted, query string parsed into QueryDict
URLResolver.resolve(path) matches HTTP path against URLPattern list; returns ResolverMatch with view function, kwargs, namespace
Handler instantiates middleware, calls view callable with (request, *args, **kwargs), catches exceptions
View executes QuerySet operations; database queries constructed, prepared statements executed, cursor results mapped to model instances
Template string compiled to AST, Context variables injected, tag/filter nodes evaluated, output HTML/JSON serialized
Response processed through middleware in reverse order (process_response); headers set, compression applied, caching headers added
HttpResponse serialized to bytes, status + headers + body sent to WSGI/ASGI server

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central data model for all request/response handling. Middleware, views, handlers, template response, form processing all depend on HttpRequest/HttpResponse classes. Carries all client metadata through the pipeline.

Orchestrates entire request lifecycle: imports middleware, urls, views, exception handling, signals. Implements WSGI/ASGI protocol and middleware ordering. Coordinates timing of request → view → response flow.
Bridges routing layer to view dispatch. Used by handlers to resolve path to view, by template tags for reverse URL generation, by debugging tools. Central coordination point for URL space.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time, user info) and emits as JSON-compatible log records.
new_types: RequestMetadata (dataclass: view_name, query_count, response_time, user_id, status_code)
StructuredLogRecord (namedtuple or Protocol for structured output)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow MiddlewareMixin pattern from django/utils/deprecation.py. Follow async support pattern from CSRFMiddleware (process_request, process_response as sync stubs calling _process_*_async if available). Follow logging pattern from django/utils/log.py (use logging.getLogger, emit as dict via extra= param).
change_proposal.integration_point: Middleware stack processes all requests/responses. Hook process_request to capture start_time + view resolution. Hook process_response to measure end_time, capture QuerySet.query_log from connection.queries. Query logging already available via django.db.utils or via SQL_DEBUG + connection.queries_log mechanism. Emit structured record via logging.handlers supporting JSON formatters.
risks: Query logging overhead: enabling connection.queries_log for all requests in production (even filtered middleware) adds 5-10% latency; recommend opt-in via setting or decorator.
Async compatibility: process_request/process_response must remain sync; structured logging data collection must not block. Defer JSON serialization to logging handler thread.
Circular imports: middleware module may import from views or handlers for introspection; avoid importing request/response types at module level if those already import middleware.
Memory: storing QuerySet objects or full stack traces in log records can leak memory if logging handlers buffer; recommend streaming or immediate flush.
Compatibility with third-party middleware: order matters; place early in stack (before CSRF) to capture full query count, but after error handlers to avoid logging 500 responses twice.
